methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $3.5B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Focused Energy is a Series A nuclear energy company founded by Thomas Forner and Markus Roth that has raised $80M+. The valuation universe comprises 11 nuclear energy peers: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Fuse Energy, Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power.

A peer-median method proved inapplicable due to insufficient comparable data points at the same stage and valuation tier within nuclear energy. Consequently, valuation relied on stage-progression analysis, which uses $3.5B as a baseline derived from total capital raised and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to subsequent rounds. This approach yielded an implied valuation of $7.0B.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $6.0B to $8.0B, with a median of $7.0B. Key uncertainties include the appropriateness of the 2.0x multiplier given Focused Energy's specific technology maturity and market timing relative to the broader nuclear energy sector recovery. The absence of a robust comparable company set limits cross-validation of the stage-progression result and increases reliance on the single methodology applied.
